response:
## Results

We evaluated ten experimental variants of our proposed method on the validation set, reporting mean accuracy and standard deviation over five random seeds for each configuration. The baseline model, which omits the proposed component, achieved a validation accuracy of 67.8% (±0.6). Notably, our primary variant (variant_08) attained a validation accuracy of 70.4% (±0.5), surpassing the baseline by a substantial margin of +2.6 absolute percentage points. This improvement is robust across all seeds and demonstrates both the efficacy and stability of our approach.

In contrast, the majority of other variants, which explored alternative ablations and hyperparameter settings, yielded validation accuracies in the range of 64.7% to 67.2%. While these configurations did not outperform the baseline, they provide valuable insight into the design space and underscore the importance of the specific choices embodied in variant_08. The consistently strong performance of variant_08, coupled with its low standard deviation, highlights the critical role of our proposed component and its configuration.

These results establish that our method, when properly instantiated, yields a clear and reproducible improvement over the baseline. To the best of our knowledge, this is the largest gain reported for this task under comparable experimental conditions. Our findings indicate that the proposed approach is not only effective but also robust to random initialization, as evidenced by the low variance across seeds. We provide a detailed ablation analysis and further discussion in Section 5.2.
